consumer_electronics.title: Social media addiction lawsuit against Meta is dropped
consumer_electronics.summary: A closely watched social media addiction lawsuit that had been set to go to trial next week has been dropped after the plaintiff voluntarily dismissed his claims against Meta, leaving none of the major tech companies facing trial in the case.

consumer_electronics.title: SoundCloud acquires decentralized music platform Nina Protocol months after its shutdown
consumer_electronics.summary: SoundCloud has acquired decentralized music platform Nina Protocol, months after the startup announced it would shut down. The deal brings Nina’s artists, editorial archive, and music discovery tools to SoundCloud as the company continues expanding its platform for independent musicians.

consumer_electronics.title: How OpenAI’s human mistake led to the AI-powered hack on Hugging Face
consumer_electronics.summary: OpenAI made a mistake setting up what it called a “highly isolated” testing environment and sandbox. According to cybersecurity experts, that human mistake is what made the AI-powered attack on Hugging Face possible.

channel_news.title: How Bloom Nutrition is expanding internationally
channel_news.summary: This year, Bloom Nutrition has been on a growth streak, and recently entered Australia, France and the U.K. Its Vice President of Global Growth, Joel Contartese, joins the Modern Retail Podcast to discuss how the brand has adapted its marketing, positioning and products for different markets.

channel_news.title: ‘The store is a lemonade stand’: Brands are lending their retail spaces to other brands for events
channel_news.summary: Brands like P.F. Candle Co. and Sorbara’s are lending their physical spaces to other brands for guest pop-ups. The strategy helps lift foot traffic and offset real estate costs.
